name: Abbiati, Furio Alexander
description: Northern winds are never famous for their gentleness. Three years ago, however, there was a gust of unusual violence and "Abbie" blew into Durham using a laundry bag as a parachute. Those who know say that this big, handsome caveman has made numerous conquests of the fair sex. Having been unusually subtle in the concealment of his personal affairs, however, "Abbie" has in a general way succeeded in maintaining his well known air of dignified reserve. "Abbie" made a berth on the "M" team by virtue of his stellar work at the winter Carnival last year.

name: Andrews, Pauline Mae
motto: Eat, drink and be merry, for tomorrow
description: Those who know Polly best claim that her laugh should be patented, or caged — the only one in existence. We wonder — is there a skeleton in her closet — she claims haughty indifference to mortal man, although they are rather handy to "trip the light fantastic" with. Somewhat like geometry she is — Q.E.D., you know — and interesting — that goes without saying.

name: Anglin, John Ignatius
motto: His mirth is warrant for his welcome.
description: "Jack" is first of all a student and then he is a real jolly good fellow. "Jack" sharks all his courses and for this reason has no time for co-eds, but get him in a crowd of fellows and you'll find him a real entertainer. "Yes Sir, that's my baby," "Does your Mother know you're out, Cecelia?" "I'm knee deep in Daisies" and "Head over heels in love" are favorites in "Jack's" repertoire and seem to argue that "Jack" has amours along the North Shore of which we know nothing.

name: Atwood, Eleanor Elizabeth
motto: A winning way, a pleasant smile.
description: Anyone as good-looking as "Peanut" doesn't need to be anything else, but she is. She can swim, play hockey and basketball. She was made class secretary by an unanimous vote this year, and is on the GRANITE board. She has personality plus and a sense of humor that never fails. This record has omitted one of her activities — you guessed it — co-education.

name: Ayers, Lester Charles
motto: Behold, a mighty Spanish Athlete, Marvel at the bull he throws.
description: Although "Les" is not a physical giant he makes up his loss with an abundance of the "gray matter." "Les" is an honor student in spite of his many activities. His only regret is that "Lou" came before him. "Les" was the cutest little Miss—(take) in "Purple Passion," the seven-reel melodrama staged by Captain Pettee with a cast from the football team.

name: Baldwin, Howard Bradford
description: "Bunger" is rather a quiet chap and one gets the idea that he is a little slow, but a few girls that know him—well, they don't believe it. We feel that he got slightly stung on an antique flivver deal, but he is a good sport and everyone likes him. Just put the word modest and that big word Wilton together and you have "Bunger" characterized.

name: Beeler, William Francis
high school: B. M. C. DURFEE HIGH SCHOOL
description: Since "Bill" left Fall River most of the populace has moved to other places. We have a suspicion that "Bill" was an indirect cause. Now he writes to Providence, Worcester, and Manchester. In his preparation to study law he has taken almost every course possible including Geology and Zoology. Glee Club and Music are "Bill's" favorites. He would have liked Soccer, but Mr. Swasey's opposition was too much for him so he has changed to letter-writing.

name: Berry, Norman Jonathan
description: Appearances are deceitful. Now this youth peering at you in the picture has all the ear and thumb marks of a student; but we again state, appearances are deceitful. It is rumored that the heart of an Exeter lass skips six or seven beats every time his name is mentioned, but of course this is only a rumor. His personal energy and his good nature are gifts indeed to be coveted.

name: Beattie, Robert Archibald
description: A moonlight night, a leafy bower, a brunette, — but why continue? It is enough to know that our fell and fell flat. He claims that the best thing about Durham is the 2:16 train for Somersworth. With aspirations of becoming the mayor of Woodsville enrolled with the class of '27. But alas, — his ambition was cast to the high winds for he slipped on a banana peel. As sings his plaintive, heart-rending chant, "To a Grecian Urn," we haven't a thing to say against him.

name: Blodgett, Marguerite Lillian
description: Rita liked Durham so well her freshman year that she moved down here and became one of the telephone operators. However, we've noticed that she still spends her vacations in Henniker, and we have an idea that she will return there after finding that teaching home-economics isn't all it's cracked up to be.

name: Blum, Leopold Bernard Jr.
description: Someone asked Leo what kind of a car he drove. He replied, "You know those nice Lincoln sedans that Henry Ford makes? Well I drive one of those cars that Henry Ford makes too." Ike started his college career in the Freshman Palace, Barracks "B," and he had an ambition to be an electrical engineer. According to him, he still has the same ambition, so we wish you luck, Leo.

name: Boyd, James Alexander
motto: Small in stature, but mighty in spirit
description: "I come from Dedham." With these great words from his lips, James Alexander made his debut at New Hampshire. See what Mellin's Food has done for this bouncing baby, and the girls all think he is just too cute for words. So that settles it. He must be. We should like to go into details about his many adventures but space is not allowed, and so his picture will have to do.

name: Brady, Helen
description: Buses don't mean a thing to Helen. She can't walk even when the clock is striking twelve. If you don't think she is a good sport, just ask her about it. Of course, the company and the refreshments mean a lot. We know from experience that no one will ever put anything over on Helen. Whatever she attempts in her life she will succeed in, and we all wish the best of luck.

name: Breen, Daniel Francis
description: This quiet, yet happy-go-lucky lad from the Bay State was not long in showing his ability in the art department. Right now his only rivals are Earle Christy, Coles Phillips and a few other noted artists. When not engaged with pen and brush he is usually playing his trumpet and he sure can blow red-hot syncopation. On top of his artistic and musical temperament he has a keen sense of humor and a ready wit that makes him well liked by those who know him best.

name: Bryant, Burnell Varnum
description: "Bunny" hails from New York Military Academy and he can show the "would be" officers in the R. O. T. C. some snappy stuff. The band has not sounded so loud lately, for "Bunny" is leading it instead of playing that trombone in the second row. "Bunny" used to commute to Mt. Holyoke but has decided that Beaver, Pennsylvania, is a bit far for week-end trips now. He is the best drilled soldier in the unit and we are all sure that he will make a snappy officer in the army before long.

name: Bryant, William Jr.
description: "Bill" blew in from Manchester with a jar of cold cream in one hand and a bottle of hair tonic in the other. These he has applied with vigor ever since. He has chartered a room in the Barracks for the full four years. He started out to give the athletic teams a little time his Freshman year, but since then he has had one of the fairer sex that takes up all his spare moments. So he says that he really cannot spend the time to go up and see the Coaches. He gets his phys. ed. travelling between the Barracks and THE girl's dormitory. "Bill" is a good-natured fellow and everybody likes him.

name: Brydon, Lloyd Harris
motto: Bride is a quiet boy from Portland, who never wastes any time on co-eds; yet at the social functions he is there with a snappy import. Bride is a fellow we all like and we hope he will make his mark in life and succeed in the turbulent waters of matrimony. Because of his quiet manner his value has not been appreciated, but among men watch his smoke.

name: Burnham, Robert Francis
motto: Bob came to Durham in '23, well equipped with city airs. Soon he became envied by his classmates for his ability to obtain help and information from the instructors. His attention was little attracted by the co-ed, but amiable news was regularly received from North Andover.

name: Burpee, Dorothy Follansby
motto: Since coming to Durham, Dot has not been known to waste a single minute. She sharks every course - especially History courses dealing with the Normans and Anglo-Saxons in England. But our Dot does not devote all her time to studies. Every Wednesday after Convocation a big car rolls up to Smith Hall - and - Well, we won't tell tales out of school - We know, don't we, Dot?

name: Burpee, Willena Florence
motto: Willena is often entertained by 'royalty,' but still remains the demure young lady that she was when she first landed from Newport. Everyone knows and loves 'Willy.' Why shouldn't they? She can do just about everything. Playing soccer and dancing are just a few of her pet diversions. Space prevents us from enumerating more; let it suffice to say that we consider 'Willy' an all 'round good sport.
clubs: Women's Student Government Association, Treasurer (2), Secretary (3)

name: Calderwood, Donald Cameron
description: "Caldy" comes from Nashua, the home of athletes, of both the Spanish and American varieties. During his first two years, he used to go up to Memorial Field and stretch his legs once in a while, and he sure can tear off a quarter in short order. But Don's mainstay is his "line" with which he lays low both students and faculty, male and female. We hear that he plans to enter politics and don't doubt that he'll make good.

name: Carli, Armando Ralph
description: "Pat" began his career in the Barracks where he was "Monarch of all he surveyed." He learned to swing a mean left, followed with a meaner right. If you don't believe it get in the way of one. He can also swing a mean vocabulary, and many a prof. has "bit the dust," under "Pat's" bombardment of heavy English. He made the English society and could have made the Aggie Club too, because he's right there with the "bull." He has daily practice work in composition from the girl in Malden and he daily has to correct or at least look over her two contributions to the higher literature.

name: Carpenter, John Thurston
description: The Carpenter turned Architect. John is one of those quiet boys who says little, works hard, and always gets there. He will manage varsity basketball next year, and some of the stenogs who eat at the Commons say that he is a good man in any kind of a position.

name: Cassily, Catherine Mary
description: We wonder about Kaer - a study in contrasts. You might think she is a quaint medieval type until you watch her, alert and poised in a basketball game. And perhaps you think she is quiet until you hear of her fun in Dover and Boston. We give up trying to solve the mystery - we know we like her and that's enough.

name: Caswell, Maurice Harold
description: Maurice comes from the great Bow Lake Country. He went to Austin Cate for his preliminary training, and was the "Sheik" of the school. Whenever the dog sleds are running on time he goes home week-ends. Everybody likes Maurice, and believe me, he can blow his horn — in the band.

name: Chase, Charles Elroy
clubs: Regimental Sergeant Major, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Chasey," the boy from the North country, certainly knows how to chase 'em, whether in the classroom or on the campus. His first year was one of many successes, but the second, the co-eds made a big hit on "Chasey." Nevertheless his natural talent still shows up in his work at all times. Only time will tell which is the stronger.

name: Chipman, Walter Albert Jr.
description: "Chipie's" ambition is to be a doctor some day. When he finishes his course and qualifies as a doctor, he is going to establish his headquarters at Manchester and treat his "love-sick patients" with his newly discovered medicine, "The Bohunk Love Varnish." After having obtained great fame from this new discovery and after having cured thousands of broken hearts, he is going to start a new research work on a cure for "Old Age." Then, after having changed all grandfathers and grandmothers into naughty college boys and flappers, he will settle down and live the life of a "gentle doctor."

name: Clark, Frank Kenneth
high school: Canaan High School, Kimball Union Academy
motto: "Ice cream tonight, boys?"
description: From the wilds of Canaan came Clarkie. He concealed himself in Barracks B for his first year. When he returned for his second year he started out to make his fortune by selling ice cream in the dorms. His nightly trips have made him famous as a prominent salesman. Now he is living in Fairchild, and says if the boys keep on buying ice cream he will soon own a hall of his own.

name: Clark, George Henry
motto: Week-End
description: Clark has never yet signed up for a course which falls on Friday or Saturday. On those days he is playing the part of the "Big Blond Brute" at Oak Manor, Coolidge Corner, and other well known resorts. He delights in smashing down bedroom doors and other obstacles which block his Primrose Path. George is all right and he means well.

name: Clark, Leslie Martin
description: "Les" has left us but he will not be forgotten. He blew in from Manchester on a break rod, and was a stirring rod on the campus while he was here. "Les" was one of those boys who never let his studies interfere with his fairer companions; but a better lad than "Les" is hard to find. He was a good sport and a hard worker. His services to the class will never be forgotten.

name: Coe, Helen Jewell
description: One fine day in September in the year of our Lord, nineteen hundred and twenty-three, Helen formed an hypothesis, mathematically speaking, that she had remained in the background long enough, and that it was time for her to take her place among the shining lights of the world. She therefore picked up her carpet bag and landed in Durham on the 8:06. If the saying is true that "first impressions are lasting" Helen must have formed a wonderful impression of that train, for she has come in on it nearly every morning since.

name: Colby, Nathaniel Henry
motto: Number Please
description: "Nat" is pictured to most of us as a great Duke as he rides along in that good car that he owns. Between classes he takes his position on a stool and plugs in calls at the Telephone Office for the despondent boys who wish to communicate with the fairer sex. If he does not fall asleep he usually gets the party for us. "Nat" has now gone to Norwich to train for the army. He is a good-hearted lad and we will always remember him.

name: Coleman, Charles David
description: "Charlie's" time is split four ways: going to college, commuting to Rochester, with his girl, and living in — at home. Let's see, now; when he goes to college he studies, when he commutes he rides, when he is at home he runs a hotel and — er — 'nuff said.

name: Colovos, Nicholas Filip
description: "Nick" is a good example of what hard work and a couple of fast fists will do. He says he got through high school in two years — we wonder what they kicked him out for — and is smashing his way through college. If he doesn't snap to in Mathematics, he will not graduate an Aggie Chemist in time to order his casket. During the summer he serves dish-water coffee in a New York restaurant, and in the winter serves his wicked right on the boxing team. Stick to it, "Nick," but leave Jamaica Plain girls alone.

name: Connor, Clyde Cedric
motto: "I'm Innocence personified."
description: Clyde's first ambition was to become a professional jockey, but the gray mare threw him as well as his dreams in a snowdrift. He seems to have very little use for our dear co-eds, but the certain party happens to be teaching school in a Massachusetts city. However, he has been known to take a co-ed out "just to oblige his roommate." His extra curriculum activities consist of the following: pastor emeritus at the Community Church, and chief "bull" thrower of the junior class.

name: Cook, Charles Atkinson
motto: "Great oaks from little acorns grow." — Webster
description: A forester there was — but is no more. Upon discovering that it takes forty years for a pine tree to mature, he, being of a restless nature, forsook the sylvan glades of our college woods for the acid-reeking research laboratories of the "chemikers." He has hopes that in his research among the scarce dirt he will discover some new rare element which will rival radium.

name: Cotton, Harold Parker
motto: Oh Captain, my Captain.
description: Although "Kid" began his college days with the class of 1926 he decided to extend his stay and elected to join the glorious class of 1927. Most of his time is taken up by a fair co-ed — and she is really fair. However, in off moments Cotton found time to be "Prexy" his Freshman year, play basketball two years and captain the team his Junior year. As he shoots towards the "hoop" you can see every co-ed in the balcony murmuring — "Oh Captain, my Captain."
text:
Oh Captain, my Captain.
Although "Kid" began his college days with the class of 1926 he decided to extend his stay and elected to join the glorious class of 1927. Most of his time is taken up by a fair co-ed — and she is really fair. However, in off moments Cotton found time to be "Prexy" his Freshman year, play basketball two years and captain the team his Junior year. As he shoots towards the "hoop" you can see every co-ed in the balcony murmuring — "Oh Captain, my Captain."

name: Courser, Edith Jeannette
motto: Five feet two, eyes of blue, But oh! what can't those five feet do.
description: Mighty little, we should say, when the quotation is applied to Edith Jeannette. Aside from writing poetry, "cracking" books and winning class numerals, she still has found time to spend a little while with a certain young man on this campus. Although she seems very sober and demure to the outside world, her friends know her as a person gifted with an unusual sense of humor. Her "yarns" about Warner have held more than one audience spellbound until the wee small hours of the morning.

name: Craig, Ralph Birry
motto: Love is not love, which alters when it alteration finds.
description: "Sam" was named for the village constable. Despite the influence of his constabulary predecessor, however, he has already broken seven of the Ten Commandments. With great aspirations of becoming a musician "Sammie" has tried his skill at cymbals, harps, and kazoos, but has been most successful as a Victrola enthusiast. Being an ardent reader of Shakespeare he has adopted for his slogan, "Love is not love, which alters when it alteration finds."

name: Crane, Charles
description: "Charlie" came to us as a transfer from Bucknell, and we were lucky. He is as capable as they make them in anything from hockey to basketball or indoor sports. "Charlie" used to have a woman but like the rest of us he learned, and now is a profound woman-hater. "Charlie" may not be the best student in the world but he doesn't have to worry about passing his courses. If there is anything that he wouldn't at least try to do for anyone, we would like to hear about it.

name: Curtis, Harry Melville Jr.
high school: Exeter Academy, Swampscott High, Huntington School
description: "Tim" visited three prep schools and then decided to come to college. His success as an Aggie is very promising. We are undecided as to what "Tim" does with all his spare time. We are not sure which one, or we might say, which ones are the lucky co-eds. He comes with the reputation of Swampscott sheik. He has held a place on Coach Cowell's football squad for two years. "Tim" is an easy-going lad and will help anyone whenever he can. We all wish you the best of luck, "Tim."

name: Day, John Woodberry
description: "Rainy" Day has been to college three years and he does not yet know who, why or what for. Thanks to "Johnnie" there has never been a shortage of Managers in the gymnasium. He has aspirations of being a C. P. A. and is taking it out on "Robbie" the plumber.

description: Encouraged by success in his native haunts "Jake" started to "Weare" his way through college. name: Dearington, Searls
motto: "What a difference clothes make in a man."
description: "Lord" Dearington is a real snappy collegiate dresser. If ever you are in doubt as to what is fashionable just consult the "Lord." "Dearie" has finally found a companion who is also interested in Russian Literature. "There is no such thing as love," is "Dearie's" favorite soliloquy. And, again "Love is a dynamic illusion." Yet this young man proceeds to be most faithful to the fair sex in general and, of course, one in particular. All great young minds work at cross purposes anyway.

name: Derby, Carl Calvin
description: Here is a very quiet chap who acts, but talks very little. He is a mean "Bill" Tilden the second, when it comes to swinging a tennis racket, and speaking of rackets, you should hear him play the sax. Just because he comes from Peterboro and is such a quiet lad is nothing against him. He can hit the books without touching them and this goes to show that success is in store for him, even if the store is the one his father is now running.

name: Dicey, Irving Tilton
description: One fine morning in September, 1923, the shark from Pinkerton Academy grabbed the 8:30 out of Derry and arrived in Durham where he at once reported to "Dad" Henderson who assigned him a room in the "lowly Barracks." He started as an engineer, but later shifted to Liberal Arts. His major at present is English, and under the able tutelage of "Jock" Sanborn he is proving very efficient in his courses. Every Saturday morning at 11:00 the Derry Express stops at the Barracks and "Dice" takes the wheel and heads for the sticks. No one knows what there is to entice him away from the beauties of Durham, but like grapenuts, "There's a reason."

name: Dimock, Morris Wilton
description: "Ickey" percolated to Durham from the Navy Yard town. Many do not realize his athletic ability, but he is the champion cross country man at "Marshall's Cafe." He is a modest gent and somewhere there is a woman who takes a lot of his time. He has become a good hash slinger and he also throws other things around. He doesn't appear to be as studious as one might think he is, but he hits the marks for high averages. He is a lovable boy and everybody who knows him likes him.

name: Dodge, Carolyn Ella
description: "K" really isn't as sedate as she suggests on first sight. You'd hardly recognize her as the same Carolyn who bade New Boston a fond farewell three years ago. Her chief interest is in Pi Gamma, but we wonder if all of her work there is in the line of zoological research.

name: Dolan, Mary Agnes
motto: Mary seems to go by opposites. She looks like a very sociable, jolly sort, but everyone says she is altogether too shy and bashful. We say they don't really know her. There is one sensible thing about her - she doesn't give a rap for the men. All her spare time goes to keep the quiet proctors in Smith Hall busy.

name: Dustin, Ralph Clement
motto: Although some doubt it, 'Ben' is a descendant of the terrible Hannah Dustin. Others say he is a living advertisement for Palm Olive Soap - 'Keep that School Girl Complexion.' It is agreed that school has not created, nor has 'Ben' inherited, any war-like characteristics, even if he is a lady-killer. If it was not for him the church would not be kept warm. His quiet and modest ways cannot help but make people like him, and some day he will be engineering a great project for dear old Penacook. Be careful, 'Ben,' don't get too high from the ground.

name: Dyment, Ray
motto: 'Hello,' says 'Red' when you wake him up in the morning. 'Hello-hello.' He still thinks he is sleeping in a hammock on the ship. He loves to rave about China and even though he doesn't like it, get him to talk about it. Where he hasn't been isn't worth going. He hasn't been to Concord recently. 'Now over in China,' - he'll say, or 'When I was in Singapore,' - but you ought to see the women in gay Paree ... Don't let him get started or you're in for an all-night session.

name: Eastwood, Medora Viola
motto: 'Med' is an ideal girl scout and we firmly believe that she really observes all the Girl Scout laws. 'Med' is also the only real man-hater on record who means what she says. But if you want a true friend, go to 'Med.'

name: Eaton, Hazel Winnifred
motto: ".... and twenty-five cents for the movies."
description: Hazel is an ardent fan and her budget always includes the movies. "Zel" is quite a little math shark, too. We often hear her figuring—"Now, I've taken three cuts—one more would make four. Oh dear! I wish I hadn't cut last time, and then I could cut to-day." She says these cuts make you work better when you do go to class, and her actions prove her words. We wonder if Hazel hasn't a few "Ties" at home, because she rather ignores the sheiks around Durham.

name: Fairchild, Frances Faith
motto: "Where are you going, Frances?" We ask. "Oh, another darn committee meeting," is invariably the answer.
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Social Committee (1), (2)
description: She's always on them—committees and committees and committees. But she saves Friday evenings for—oh you all know. He's a tall, thin man with a Jewish accent. Someday when you are attending one of those brilliant social events in Washington, someone will ask "Who is that blond, dignified person in the gorgeous gown?"—and you will remember Frances of college days.

name: Farr, Annie Gertrude
description: When she came here Gertrude enrolled as a "Home Wrecker," but of late she has shown alarming signs of becoming a "Chem shark." We don't quite know about Gertrude. She has been seen often with the fastest man on the campus; at least the "best in the long run," and he's a member of the royalty at that. We wonder if chemistry is more practical than Home Ec. after all.

name: Farrar, Elbert Raymond
motto: "Spud" is a quiet fellow, but as the saying goes, "Still waters run deep."
description: All we have to judge him by is his excellent scholastic ability, which has won for him the admission into two honorary fraternities. If Farrar has had any connection with the fair sex he has been quite successful in concealing his emotions. However, we believe there is a great future ahead of him.

name: Fitch, Alice Lila
motto: Al's favorite sports are indoor. She loves to read and 'sit and sew a fine seam,' and she always knows the latest news on campus. We wonder if she stays inside to protect that peaches and cream complexion of hers. We also wonder if she finds that the night air does it good. We don't know what her ambition is, but we've heard that she intends to be a school teacher.

name: Flynn, Dorothy
motto: Dot is far from pepless — just see where she comes from. She never seems to be able to stick to one person, place or thing for very long (it is a woman's privilege to change her mind) — although she has remained fairly constant to a certain popular seaside resort. Dot is a good sport always and you can't stump her. Another characteristic is that Dot is some little stepper. Her good disposition and friendly ways will gain success for her we are sure.

name: French, Wilford Albion
motto: Well, what of it? Frenchie broke into college athletics by captaining the 1927 baseball club. He sure is a mean man behind the bat. He says he will be an engineer; perhaps he will, who knows? But all in all, Frenchie can study, play baseball, and think; a prince and a friend to all.

name: Frost, Lore Alford
motto: I'm modest and I haven't told you half what I really am. Frosty has a girl and he admits it. Who can she be? He hasn't shown us yet. Frosty came down from Pinkerton, and still admits his prep school sheik ability won him fame. He's a busy man; he likes to work as will be seen in Frosty's Athletic Section of the GRANITE. He does all he sets out to do well. A good fellow and conscientious, characterizes Frosty.

name: Gale, Marjorie Harriet
motto: Marge went to Jackson during the first part of her college career but for several reasons she decided to come to this university. Marge appears to be a very quiet little girl until you know her — then you realize she's the best sport ever. We often wonder why she always looks so happy when she returns from one of her Tilton trips, and where she got the ring with the strange seal on it.

name: George, Charles Adna
motto: Here we have a real 'Aggie' who has one big aim in life, namely, to get married and become principal of the Contoocook Grammar School. Bud came here with a good understanding of Baseball, Basketball and Croquet; but after being here a short time he decided that Smith Hall has greater attractions. His outlooks are good and he is sure to succeed in whatever he starts.

name: Gerrish, Grace Elizabeth
motto: Most people think that Grace is a Home-Ecker but we know that she is majoring in French. She is a very efficient business woman and expends her energies in conducting social activities in Madbury.

name: Gill, Maclean John
motto: Mac took the dog sled from the far North and slipped into Barracks 'A' where he spent his first year of college. Here, before the year was over, his alarm clock and his hard work made him famous. He has been working ever since, and is one of those fellows who comes to college to benefit the college as well as himself. There are few campus activities that he has not had something to do with. If there is any, tell him and he will soon remedy matters. Did you ever see him peeved? Mac's ever-ready, cheerful smile and cordial 'Hi,' with a good word to and for all surely predict a prosperous road in the future for him.

name: Goodrich, John Gardner
description: "Johnny" is our quiet product from Exeter until he gets hold of his "horn" - then goodbye quiet. Although he had to drop out of the game for a while on account of sickness he is back again to get his degree. Based on the time he has spent with the B. & M. while commuting, John is going to apply for the position on the engine where he can toot his horn to the accompaniment of the whistle.

name: Hall, Florence Ellen
description: Florence is minoring in Home Economics so that she may be able to do things in the "proper" way. In fact, she has become quite a culinary artist since she has been with us. She gave no college except Dartmouth any time until the third term of her Freshman year, when she centered her attention on N. H. U. Hats off to the only woman on campus who does not disagree with her room-mate. She sincerely believes that although it may be wonderful to be silver-tongued, silence is golden.

name: Harris, Gladys Annie
description: Gladys is another of these quiet girls who do everything. It would be far easier to list the classes in which she does not shine than to list those in which she is brilliant. All her time doesn't go for books either - although a certain "Page" gets considerable of it.

name: Harrison, Harry Lincoln
description: H. Lincoln Harrison, Senior, late by one year in the GRANITE, late to classes for two years, late in arriving at New Hampshire by two years. He completed fifteen years of school at ten different schools, not including Sunday Schools. He became a passionate art lover at Penn. College, Oskaloosa, Iowa, where his Mathematics professor pronounced him good at figures. His reputation as a sheik at Harvard has grown with his transfer to New Hampshire, where his derby, spats, enormous collection of toilet articles, his Quaker disposition, and his utility as a transportation expert have daily increased his popularity.

name: Hartshorn, Pearl Editha
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Pearl is a hustler and is always on deck when it comes to studies or outside activities. She is always ready for fun and a good laugh. In spite of her work she finds plenty of time to study the social side of life. Hartsie is always ready to lend a helping hand to everyone. Once in a while she leaves us for a week-end, but we feel sure that her "interest" is at N.H.U.

name: Hayden, Leslie Forrest
description: "Chick" is a big, husky, good-hearted "kid" from Newfields. He tends toward Horticulture, with a leaning towards flowers. "Chick" is another unfortunate commuter. He would have made his numerals and probably his letter if he had not been commuting. "Chick" is one of the best fellows you can ever hope to meet. His generosity will always be remembered. His one bad trait is kidding his fellow townsmen, but we can't blame him for that. He is just one of the boys, and one that we will always remember.

name: Heald, Benjamin
motto: "Five feet — two" —
description: This little fellow arrived in the fall of twenty-three with all other little "Frosh" and immediately made it known on the ground floor of Fairchild that he was from Holderness and didn't care who knew it. For two terms "Bennie" made weekly visits to all surrounding towns but then he fell for a — woman. The weekly visits ceased and nightly pilgrimages to the Alpha Xi House started. The kid is never known to study but he hits his courses for a million. How he can get away with it is more than anyone can tell. If anyone can get along in the world we are willing to wager that "Bennie" can.

name: Henault, Norman Joseph
description: "Buck" is a quiet and unassuming chap. He is always busy and always happy. With a car like that and a girl like that, who could not be happy? If efficiency means anything in the world, count on "Buck's" success. He does everything in a business-like way and knows that if a thing is well done one must do it himself. Besides running all the dances for his fraternity, editing his fraternity paper and filling the position of Photographic Editor on THE GRANITE, "Buck" keeps his average way up in the list and gets his name posted with the sharks.

name: Hill, Margaret Evelyn
high school: Franklin High School, Franklin, N. H.
motto: A merry heart goes all the way
description: It takes a long while to get acquainted with "Marg" but if you haven't known her you have missed something. She is heaps of fun and as a friend she is true blue. "Marg" is one of the best dancers in our class, and as a forward in basketball she can't be beat. What chance has she for a career, handicapped as she is by her dimples and those black eyes!

name: Hixon, Stanley Radcliffe
description: "Stan" has made himself conspicuous since our Freshman year by his nervous temperament, golden locks and that grin. While he doesn't own a car he seldom walks when cars are able to get around. With his numerous social activities "Swede" has found time enough to glean much fruit from the tree of knowledge, as well as expose himself to the appreciation of music. Before coming to New Hampshire, "Stan" went to the Southwest and drifted north with the harvest. He talks most interestingly about the Knights of the road and the nights on the road. His wealth of material for a background can be attached to this great adventure.

name: Hoagland, William Lloyd
description: "Bill" is majoring in History largely because he is such an admirer of Alexander. He says that maybe Robert Fulton didn't find the Claremont to be successful; he finds that there is something of real value in the Claremont. Who is she, "Bill?" "Bill" is one of New Hampshire's most popular students. His good nature, ready wit and cheerful smile make him admired by everyone on the campus. Good luck is the wish of all of '27 to you, "Bill."

name: Hodge, Lucille Clark
high school: Concord High School, Concord, N. H.
description: Lucille came from Concord, but straight from high school, and not from that institution for which the city is noted. She frequently receives letters with foreign post-marks. Consequently, her friends are trying to solve the conundrum - Why is she majoring in Latin if she is going to France? Her favorite pastime is telling about the thrilling experiences which she had one summer in Vermont, and we hear of many more which took place in Wolfeboro, but she refuses to commit herself when questioned about the latter.

name: Holt, Clarence Dodge
description: They call him "Smiling Sam, the sheik of Alabam." But there is nothing sheikish in the makeup of this sunny-dispositioned, auburn-headed disciple of "King" James. He hails from New Boston over in the wilderness west of Manchester. He prefers chemistry to co-eds and doesn't give the latter any time. "Sam" does not make much noise but an indication of his ability is shown by his acceptance into the Honorary Chemical Fraternity.

name: Holt, Esther
description: Esther's middle name was efficiency until she became quiet proctor on the first floor of Congreve. Then it was found necessary to campus her for two weeks to make her realize her position. She is sharking the accounting course and plans to be a clerk in the Suncook Bank.

name: Hopkins, Walter Scott Jr.
description: Sir Walter fell for Peter Pan, but she flew out of the window with Wendy, and since then "Hoppy" has been talking to himself and managing the cross country runners. Walter usually knows what day it is and he is happiest when Proctor beats Tilton.

name: Horne, Roger Bigelow
motto: "Just because you are a big guy"...
description: "Shorty" came into town on a half ticket and felt insulted when "Dad" suggested that the kindergarten was on the Dover road. After registering he soon attained the name of being the "hardest" man in Fairchild. Roger had aspirations of becoming an officer in the Reserves, but the army refused to lower the height standard to three feet four inches, so the boy has now decided to be a big business man instead. "Shorty" is a fine fellow and cannot help being liked by everyone, for he is white as they make them.

name: Hourihane, Cecilia Marie
motto: Oh, how 'Ciel' loves to get up in the early dawn at the beach. Honestly, you couldn't get her to bed before she would actually want to get up again. She loves diving too - she just glories in sporting in the sea-green waves. Yes, 'Ciel' will surely be glad when she can go 'beaching' again - slicker and all.

name: Hourihane, Ellen Wren
motto: Ellen is a demure, quiet commuter from Somersworth, but we know 'still waters run deep.' Is she slow? She may be, but when it comes to landing a shot, Ellen can hit the mark squarely every time. Her ambition in life is to arise early enough in the morning to arrive at our University in time for a certain eleven o'clock class. This young lady is seriously considering majoring in Zoo. Isn't that right, Ellen?

name: Humphrey, Helen
motto: Ten years from now Helen will be a social service worker in Ipswich, running playgrounds, food sales and such, and benefiting by Dean French's teachings. Helen can run a Ford, too, and even Stars that aren't her own if she's determined on getting somewhere - like Bow Lake. She and her roommate started for New York one Wednesday afternoon and didn't quite make Dover - they missed the bus. But Helen says they will do it sometime, so they probably will.

name: Hunt, Anna Calvert
high school: WESTMINSTER COLLEGIATE INSTITUTE, SALT LAKE CITY, UTAH
motto: Anna thinks that eight o'clock classes should be abolished, also any other class that interferes with pleasure trips. Anna's chief difficulties are finance and Dr. Allen's French courses. She is in favor of refusing permission for mid-year graduations, her argument being that they inconvenience others.

name: Hunt, Henry
description: Although "Lefty" spends his week-ends in Saugus, Mass., he certainly gives his studies adequate attention. Among his student friends he has already won distinction in the field of Architecture. His drawings are worthy of merit. There is no doubt that he will be a very successful Architect. His reserved, unselfish nature wins for him many steadfast friends.

name: Ingalls, Bernice
description: "Bunny" came to us from Tufts this year and we learn to our sorrow that she intends to return there next fall. She is one of the few people you can dignify with the title of "good all-around scholar," and her ability to spark English courses is fast becoming notorious. "Bunny" is hard to become acquainted with, but a lasting friend to the lucky ones she really likes. Here's hoping she decides to remain with us next year.

name: Jenkins, Ralph Richards
description: "Jenks" has an "N.H." and now he is after his "W" (Wheaton). Once he started for Norton in a nice collegiate Ford and went - six miles in it. He's an "Aggie" and a good one if the jewelry he wears means anything. He has one weakness and that's - women. Before more is said we'd better stop. "Jenks" is a fine sport and an all-round good fellow.

name: Johnson, Barney George
description: "Bjarne" came touted as a ski jumper; he ended as manager of football, this GRANITE and several other things. He will be remembered as the man who beat the point system by 70 or 80 points. That tired look which he wears some mornings is caused by correspondence which keeps him up long after the other boys have gone to bed.

name: Johnson, Paul Shattuck
motto: "Doc" alias "Swede" is the type of man who spends his summers on a deserted island off the coast of Maine just to show people how independent he really is.
description: His desire and ability to argue has finally landed him on the debating team. It is rumored that several cosmetic companies are trying to learn the secret of this young man's complexion. He claims that he is "off" co-eds, but his fraternity brothers do not believe everything that he says.

name: Jones, Helen Gwendolyn
motto: When not following the smoky trail of the elusive nicotine, Gwen can always be found arguing the third floor out of house and home.
description: That's why the debating coach gave her a stellar role. Whenever an unusual disturbance is heard you can safely wager that it's Gwen shushing 'em up.

name: Jordan, Harlan Carl
motto: After leaving High School, "Harl" went to work in an office. He soon became tired of sweeping and so left the land of skis to go to college.
description: Being architecturally inclined, he took that course and soon he covered his walls with work of art. Besides being an exponent of art, I know that he would "walk a mile for" -- no, not a Camel -- for a dance. I've heard that he goes to a Portland hospital for heart treatment. After one of these trips, he happened to describe the treatment in his sleep, and what a treatment it was. It is a bad habit to talk in your sleep, "Harl," you must stay at home.

name: Kalijarvi, G. Bernhard
motto: "Kal" has not been with us long. He transferred from Clark and we all feel that he is quite an asset to '27.
description: His appearance in our gym last year proves that he is a mean man, when it comes to dropping that ball in the hoop. "Kal" is a hard worker and we feel that he will be a big help to us the next two years. We all wish him the best of luck for we know that nothing less than success is in store for him.

name: Keenan, Alice Julia
description: Perhaps "Al's" temperament goes a long way in explaining why we all like her. She is seemingly serious and stern, but appearances are not always sure signs. Who ever saw "Al" grouchy, even when she as head "husher" in Smith Hall, had warned a noisy individual for the forty-eleventh time? Besides being able to maintain law and order "Al" has shown herself to be very talented in the art of impersonation.

name: Kelly, Ethel Etta
description: Ethel seems rather quiet but you ought to see her fight when she plays basketball. The forwards really haven't much chance. Ethel is a history shark besides, and manages to do pretty well in Mr. Babcock's courses. Her first year here she had some trouble with the "Pettee kids," but that is all over now. It is rumored that the Men's Club simply couldn't have their dinners without Ethel's Parker House rolls.

name: Kelsea, Oscar George
description: "Kel" came to us from the North Country and he sure has made his education worthwhile. He is an old standby in basketball, being noted for his cool-headedness. His term grades show that he is successful in his classes as well as in athletics, although he rarely sees a book. It is said that in a mid-term examination a professor asked what the name of the textbook was and George didn't have the slightest idea. However, this cannot be held against him for he has the ability to accumulate and hold knowledge. He is one of the best known and best liked men on the campus and is a chap of whom the North Country may well be proud.

name: Kennedy, Mary Josephine
motto: Gather ye rosebuds while ye may.
description: When Mary left Somersworth for Durham she was a blushing, shy young miss, but oh—what changes are wrought by college. She just dotes on house parties and informals. Mary will leave a trail of broken hearts behind her when she leaves Durham, for what young swain has escaped her winning smiles and coy glances.

name: Keough, George Harland
motto: George blew into Durham on the B. & M. and stepped onto the platform with a grip in one hand and a dime in the other. George handed the porter his grip, put the dime in his pocket and started for the Barracks.
description: He is now our leading magnate in the fur industry. Business isn't as good as it was because half of his traps is broken, but he expects to have it fixed soon. He is the King Cole of Barracks "B" and his ready wit causes his friends to be exceedingly careful in addressing him. Nevertheless George is one of our outstanding men and it is a pleasure to have known him.

name: Killkeley, James Roy
description: Roy is one of the quiet boys of the campus. We all know the old adage of still water. "Kil's" case is no exception to that theory. As he talks but little, he gives only an equal amount of knowledge about himself. The fact that he has stayed with the College of Technology for three years leads us to think that persistence is one of many virtues. "Kil," we expect great things of you.

name: Kimball, Roy George
motto: Pure as the driven snow — but he drifted
description: Roy did stop learning new dance steps (all by himself) long enough to have his picture taken, so that all the world might see: "Roy — the dancing fool." Every night he calls up "Ma," asks how "Pa" is, then brushes his hair and teeth, reads four chapters of the Scripture and goes to bed. He surely has a "rep" for bumming cigarettes, but he is a good kid just the same.

name: Kinsman, Emma Lena
motto: Smile and the world smiles with you, Weep and you weep alone.
description: Emma certainly does do her share of the smiling in Durham and is never devoid of company. For, when Emma finds anything worth smiling about, who can resist indulging in the emotion too. It will be a dark day for this campus when Emma's cheerful smile wends its way back to Somersworth.

name: Langdell, Merritt Raymond
description: He doesn't smoke, he doesn't drink; but, Oh, how he does love the odor of sweet flowers. "Lang" learned to dance out in the far East somewhere and his terpsichorean endeavors are characteristic of an austere grace and sober dignity which in its quaintness brings to mind the stately minuet. He also does fine embroidery and spends many leisure hours in this manner. He is now learning a few simple facts about Economics, being assured they are very few and very simple.

name: Langford, Anice Elizabeth
description: Unlike most girls Betty isn't always talking, but "still waters run deep" you know. She is always ready for a good time of any sort and she loves outdoor sports. Her favorite sport on Saturday night is dancing. We heard she broke some poor boy's heart last summer but she won't talk about it so we don't know the details. This is a secret - if you want to get Betty mad tell her she looks as young as she is.

name: Larson, Norman Luther
description: The Adolph Menjou of the Junior Class. A girl in Somersworth told him that she liked "Drug Store Cowboys" and so "Duffy" made connections with Ben Hill where he has made many friends as a dispenser of sweet nothings. "Duffy" was an architect until his hand became so shaky that he thought he would go better in Doctor Rudd's classes.

name: Learned, Theda Louise
description: Theda says she is going to teach for a year, at least, after graduation, but we are convinced that she has had good training for a chemist's assistant. And then, we've heard she's minoring in Home Ec. Can there be any connection? "T-D." believes that all classes should be held in Congreve Hall, thereby saving the bother of commuting to the shops. She says that if she could have received credits for all the walking she has done, she would have graduated years ago.

name: Lee, Dana Huntley
motto: Date seems to take a liking to chickens. Now don't mistake my meaning and think I'm referring to co-eds, for I mean the two-legged kind that lay eggs. He even rooms out on the chicken farm, but he is making plenty of money. The farm is about four miles out of town and that is how he keeps in training for track. Some day you will see on every egg you buy - 'Lee's Guaranteed Eggs,' but he doesn't say what they will be guaranteed to do.

name: Lewis, Steven Asa
motto: Along in the fall of '23 an innocent little boy came down from Winchester, by Heck, with a wonderful ambition to be an engineer, and proceeded to take up his residence in the noted Barracks 'A'. He starred that year in numerous wise-cracking contests with the famous Doc. Bowler, in regard to where Steve kept his mind if not his body. We fear, however, that Steve is not the innocent little boy that he was once, but with his ever-ready smile, he has made many friends on the campus.

name: Lightbown, James Pearson
high school: B. M. C. Durfee High School
motto: 'Red' was a reporter for the Fall River Gazette until he came to Durham. Here he continued his work for two years as a reporter for The New Hampshire. He said he came to college merely to patch up the weak spots in his education. He made himself famous in the Barracks his Freshman year where he held the position of champion 'bull' thrower. Since then he has been spending his time on the soccer team in the fall, and co-eds in the spring. During the winter he keeps in trim by reading dime novels and by occasional trips to Dover. 'Red' is a likable chap and will come out on top in the end.

name: Littlefield, Ralph Batchelder
motto: Ralph ran down from Pinkerton and has been running ever since. He has been a great asset to the cross country and track team as well as the winter sports. 'Lit' has affirmed that the women do not take any of his time but we are not so sure of that. He is a quiet fellow and we all know he will make a big success in the science of Agriculture.

name: Lord, Richard Theodore
description: Who ever heard of a red-headed sheik? Here is the exception that proves the rule. His supply of face powder, rouge and lipsticks is ever plentiful. Outside of that, we think "Dick" is a fine chap, and we'd all trust him with our girl friends as far as we could see him, which does not mean to Groveton. He has not decided whether to be an engineer or a psychologist. His varied knowledge of both shows his studious aspect. Some claim he will be a great engineer, and he may fool us and be one yet. Be careful, "Dick."

name: Lovering, Marguerite
description: "Coming dear" is Peg's unfailing answer to her irate and long enduring friends. Peg is the immovable object - where, oh where, is the irresistible force? Peg is firmly convinced that logic makes the world go round, but when you put a uke in her hands, logic and seriousness leave by the nearest exit. Peg is a walking example of the power of the human eyebrows. Watch her use 'em sometime.

name: Lowenburger, Helen
description: Helen never told us exactly why she transferred from Conn. Aggie last fall but we have a theory about it. We had heard the line about cousins before anyway, so we didn't believe that. Perhaps she knew that Dr. Howes was looking for a good tennis player - not that she plays tennis herself, however. Her favorite amusements are movies, bridge, and wondering how long it will be before she is a better chemist than King James.

name: Marnoch, Margaret Shaw
description: "Peg" has a sense of humor and a contagious smile. She never stays in one place long enough to locate her. Between the New Hampshire office and the drugstore, "Peg" is always busy. She used to tell us that variety was the spice of life, but now she says there's only one. She is always taking cooking and is apparently as successful there as she is in everything else.

name: Martin, Arme Cunningham
motto: Jump, Lady, jump, I've got a net . . .
description: There is only one phrase from Arme's collection of antedates. Why is it when some people ask her if she is a member of Phi Mu Delta she always smiles - with a far-away look in her eyes? But these are days of long age. Arme likes New Hampshire better than Vermont because she is fond of "little fields" and Dover. We delight in hearing her praise her courses, especially History and Psychology.

name: Mason, Lawrence Everett
description: This son of Marlboro came to Durham to help manage the Commons, and as a side issue to go to college. After living in the Barracks for two years he became as wise as they make them, and threw over his job in the Commons. Now as Board Manager he is without an equal. "Did you say that Marlboro never turned out any good men? Why, they threw me out." "Lawny" is one of the hardest workers that we ever knew and will do anything for anyone whenever he can. He has a pleasing disposition and never gets riled, and we know that nothing but success can confront him in the future.

name: Mason, Muriel
description: Muriel comes from Keene and she's proud of it. She sharks all of Mr. Schramin's art courses and flunks Phys. Ed. She says hockey is too much work. Her favorite occupation is answering the telephone.

name: Mccarthy, Irene
description: Reenie spent her sophomore year at Seton Hill, and then came back to Durham to recuperate. She makes a very good noise proctor as she has a fixed habit of never getting the real offender, but invariably landing an innocent bystander. With the exception of this one drawback she is an extremely desirable addition to Smith Hall. Reenie specializes in basketball and MATH. Her only recreation used to be Lyceum Courses, but lately she has added skating.

name: McDonald, John Joseph
description: "Mac" is another of the "Hot Retorts." He believes in continuing one's education so he is going to travel after graduating from this institution. However, we must hand it to "Mac" when it comes to an authority on the weaker sex. Nevertheless we consider "Mac" a fine sport, even if he spends time in one of the Dover banks.

name: McDonough, John Charles
description: John blew in from Manchester three years ago with that city air that makes all boys famous. He is a hard worker and when he is not playing the part of assistant proctor on the third floor of Ballard Hall, he is working at "Bill's" where he tells the boys that the best is none too good for them. John is a quiet fellow, and meets everything with a smile. We look forward to a great future for you, John, so stick in there.

name: McGrail, Thomas Henry
motto: "While words of learned and thunderous sound Amazed the gasping mystics ranged around."
description: After watching the would-be "Joe Colleges" do their stuff in his home town for some 18 years, "Mac" decided to leave the old Drug Store where he was accustomed to mix his drinks, and commute to Durham. "Mac" believes that his poetry would cause a stir in the literary world should he publish it, but he has yet to find somebody that shares this opinion. He may be seen almost any afternoon developing himself in the art of bumming on the road to Dover.

name: McLeod, Donald Kenneth
motto: "Kisses are full of microbes, But I dearly love the little devils."
description: Coming from the hamlet of Peterboro to the flourishing town of Durham was quite a shock but our little "Cy" weathered the storm. By his Junior year he became quite a man with the chickens, and now has a flock under his care. With a smile for everyone and a helping hand as well, he is one whom we all like. His one great failure is his love for patent leather hair.

name: Menard, Annette
motto: Mais Oui, c'est Annette.
description: Formerly of Keene Normal, she is normally ours now and whole-heartedly. Annette has "ideas" about men. She has ideas about other things too. Our idea about Annette centers mainly on the fact that we'd like to have her around always. She's inspirational, petite and piquant. Mellow stringed tones — soft strains of "Souvenir" — and we remember — Annette!

name: Moody, Frank Bailey
description: Frank is a commuter and has to be up in the morning to get to his eight o'clock classes, but he always makes them on time. He is a very quiet and serious lad. His one great asset is that he is an outdoor enthusiast. He is characterized as a "plugger." This is shown by his work in Track and Cross Country. Although he has not yet made his letter we are looking for him to come through soon and best of luck to him. Since he goes home every evening, we assume that there must be some deep and dark reason for it. Woman — women? — yes, perhaps. Anyway we wish him the best of luck.

name: Mooney, Chester Ernest
motto: "Behold" — the card shark and crack tennis player.
description: Mooney once played the six spot for third hand high and took the trick — "with a roar and a clatter." We feel sure that if "Chet" spends half the time at his vocation that he spends in college playing tennis, and is as successful as he is in cards, he will sure make a name for himself. "Let us all pray together."

name: Moore, Winthrop Perkins
description: "Pop" was sort of a recluse for a couple of years. The fraternity woke him up and now you couldn't find a better gent. He may be quiet but did you see the girl he was with at Carnival? Right from New York. He may come from the great Sharon duck farms, but he is no puddle jumper. When it comes to drawing pictures — well, he keeps Art. Stewart loaded with cartoons advertising peanuts and popcorn. He drew the pretty pictures on the Carnival Programs, too. He has a nice little private collection of "pitchers." Just ask him to let you see them.

name: Moulton, Nathalie Marion
motto: If when you are passing a certain girls' fraternity house you should happen to hear sweet strains of music issuing therefrom, you'd be safe in wagering your last ten cents that it was Nathalie trying out a new piece of music which one of her fraternity sisters had just brought from home. She certainly does know how to coax pleasing tones from that piano, too.
description: But do not gather from this that she has no time for anything else but making friends with the keys. For her success on the soccer field, in French class, and in several outside activities proves the contrary to be true.

name: Mountain, Pauline Letitia
motto: What's the dirt, Polly?
description: Polly is the proctor's despair and the dormitories' delight. She is absolutely guaranteed to turn the dark cloud silver side out for even the bluest freshman. Her motto might well be "anything once and most things twice." Smith Hall has been a very different place since our "little ray of sunshine" went away, and the announcement that she is returning next fall caused a wave of rejoicing.

name: Moylan, Clare Patricia
motto: Pat is a girl one has to know to fully appreciate. She's always ready for fun and she's a darn good sport. Whether it's skating, swimming or hiking, Pat's right there. She says she's going to run a tea-room some day, but with that red hair of hers - Well, we know that she'll be a success whatever she undertakes.

name: Nagel, Charles Frederick
motto: Let love alone, and I'll be with thee
description: With no ambition in the world until he found Economics, "Freddie" came to New Hampshire in the fall of 1923 to polish off his fast accumulating wealth of culture. He has succeeded and is at present one of the most collegiate "gents" on the campus. "Freddie" just can't let the co-eds alone, though, of course, he's not to blame, for they all do their part.

name: Neville, John Patrick
description: John came up from Portsmouth to see how much work he could get into four years of college. He has a good start, and before he finishes there will be few things that he has not had something to do with. His latest accomplishment is this GRANITE, which speaks for itself. "Nibs" is the happiest when the work is piled before him. His services will never be forgotten and we wish him the very best of luck and success in the future.

name: O'Kane, Catherine Vandewater
description: Now you see her — now you don't. "Cottie" is such a hustler that one never knows where to find her. She plays an important part in the social and athletic life on the campus; in the former as a good mixer and a friend to all; in the latter on account of her invincible school spirit and love of sports. Good luck, "Cottie."

name: O'Leary, Otho Francis
description: The Newfields to Durham special pulls into the depot every morning, and a bright young "onward and upward" looking youth alights therefrom. It is our ambitious friend with the classical nickname who keeps the B. & M. from bankruptcy. He has given the English department a lot of time since his arrival and very likely some large institution of learning will in the future point with pride to his presence among its faculty. His main interest of course is in poetry and he has sincerely cultivated the ability to quote at least five lines from each author, which is of course an earmark of learning.

motto: "So I would live and love until my sun descends."
description: This expresses Dot's theory of living — the girl who comes from Gloucester-by-the-sea. She did not join our class until our sophomore year, but since then she has won a host of friends. Dot is not only a good student, sport and athlete, but a fine musician as well. We congratulate the lucky Beta of Bowdoin who has won her heart.

name: Osgood, Alice Louise
motto: What made you think I did? Ask questions? Why I never do. Really? Who told you so? How long ago? Is that so?
description: Wherever you find "Oz" you are sure to see a pair of merry hazel eyes and hear an irresistible and insuppressible giggle. Although she is quiet and reserved compared to some of us she gets there very efficiently. Besides, she knows just what to wear and how to wear it.

name: Page, Harry Oliver
description: "Boozer" has made a great success of his college career. He came to us from dirty Swampscott which place he claims is the land of milk and honey. But seriously he thinks that Manchester is the land of milk and honey now. Harry is very versatile, being represented in many organizations in the University. Although his roommates his Freshman year nearly wrecked him, he is surviving the stroke very well. His popularity and pleasing personality are shown by the fact that he has twice been elected President of his class and is President of Mask and Dagger. We surely wish Harry the best of luck and know he will make good in whatever work he takes up.

description: "Eddie" says her only distinction is in being the smallest person in the class - at least we have never heard anyone dispute her when she said so. Edna used to be interested in the medical profession (indirectly at least), but we can't tell lately and she won't. You might ask the mail table, though.

name: Paige, Catherine Eliza
description: Catherine is a "Home-ecker" who is not a "Home wrecker." She's the first word in efficiency and she's good fun when she'll give us any time. Catherine won't give much attention to romance down here, but she does smile when she finds those frequent letters on the mail table - oh yes - and that photo on her bureau.

name: Patten, Roger William
motto: Pat is some day going to startle the world by taking first place in some big Olympic feat. He has been one of the old stand-bys since his Freshman year on the Cross Country team. His career started while running to and from classes from the Barracks in his Freshman year. Pat is an Aggie and is set on finding a method of raising eyeless potatoes. His latest ambition is to be an officer in the army. Pat is an ardent woman-hater and gives them very little time, but we wonder why he stays out so long sometimes.

name: Perkins, Alice May
motto: Alice is our mysterious Titian blond who spends her week-ends in Boston. She dotes on Freshman English, Zoo, and Thelma. Doctors, men in uniform and special delivery letters are her specialty.

name: Perkins, Theodore Jacob
motto: Ted is one of the most unassuming fellows you ever met. He never bothers anybody, male or female, as far as we know. He is very clever with the books and we hope some day to see him make Phi Kappa Phi. He is a very quiet chap, but he has many friends and we all know Ted will make good.

name: Phelps, Robert Thayer
motto: Jefferson lives on. Bob hails from Jefferson, where men are men and law-abiding families partake of fresh venison in July. Between the Waumbek's golf course and waitresses, his summer is pretty well taken up. Although Bobbie has chemical ambitions and is giving up his young life to carbolic acid and ginger ale, we still have hopes for him.

high school: Berlin High School, Berlin, N. H.
motto: Is it Earle or Merle?
description: ask the Theta Chi boys since Earle's twin brother came down for a visit. "Phil" is a fast man with the co-eds in the summer time. In Durham he manages to keep busy with Mr. Schramm's courses, sandwiched in with week-ends in "Tooky."

name: Pickford, Thomas Michael
high school: Berlin High School, Berlin, N. H.
motto: All hail to "Pick," another of the boys from the "North Country."
description: "Pick" believes that last love is true love; for a year he left his Alma Mater and continued his education in the city of Independence. He did not tell us much about the school, but his actions show that he surely did learn a whole lot down in "Philly." We are given to understand that the life in Philadelphia was rather boresome to "Pick" so he found his way back to the bright lights of Durham. Welcome back, "Pick," and best of luck to you.

high school: Epping High School, Epping, N. H.
motto: We once heard an irate quiet proctor state that Helen was "the only person on the whole second floor who had enough sense to keep still for longer than five minutes."
description: Helen started her college career as a "home-ecker," but after one taste of the perfectly marvelous cake she makes, that department assured her there was nothing they could teach her - and she changed to the general arts and science course.

name: Pinkham, Rolland Francis
high school: Dover High School, Dover, N. H.
motto: "Pinky" commutes from that nearby city of Dover and is proud of it too.
description: He always starts early enough to allow for walking part of the way for he never knows when that 1903 model of a Ford will buck up on him. He has never had any great accident yet but can't trust 'em. He is a man that can blow anytime. Don't misinterpret me - he blows a trombone and it sure takes wind. "Pinky" is a good-natured fellow and we know he'll make good.

He is a man that can blow anytime. Don't misinterpret me - he blows a trombone and it sure takes wind. "Pinky" is a good-natured fellow and we know he'll make good.

name: Piper, Walter Irving
motto: Pipe is one of our oldest members, not in scholastic standing by any means but in age. He was formerly a cabinet maker. He decided to enter the teaching profession and spent two years at Portsmouth High showing the boys how to swing a wicked hammer. He is now one of 'Bob's' right hand men in our Glee Club and he hits a mean note. 'Pipe' is a splendid fellow and everyone that knows him will tell you so. He has something on the rest of us, which is evident by his spending his time between classes at the Practice House.

name: Pitts, Thomas Michael
motto: When a fellow can swing back and forth on a gate for hours and smile when he tells about it; when he can act natural when he is in love, — he is amiable, and 'Tom' can do both of these things. If you have not heard him playing the piano, you haven't heard anything yet, and even though he has not the composing fever yet, don't be surprised if you hear his 'Wicked Woman Woos' sometime.

name: Poor, Bernice Lillian
motto: Bunny's ambition is to teach Home Ec some day and, judging from the fudge she makes, we're sure she'll be a great success. Bunny will make an even better housekeeper for some nice man, though, for her room is always spic and span.

name: Potts, Sherburn Moore
motto: 'Sherb' decided that the class of '27 was better than the class of '26 so left college (on own accord) for a year to take charge of affairs (love or something) at Lincoln, N. H. He started pre-med, took a little mathematics and decided to take engineering, took a little more mathematics and decided to take Liberal Arts. His record among our fair co-ed is negligible. However, the fair sex counts in Rochester with 'Sherb.'

name: Prince, William Morris
motto: Strong in will to strive, to seek, to find, and not to yield.
description: After "Bill" had spent some time at Conn. Aggies he decided that New Hampshire would make a better Alma Mater. "Bill" rightfully deserves all the respect and friendship which his classmates hold for him. When not playing a stellar game for Coach Cowell on the right extremity of the rush line, "Bill" is chasing amoebas in Professor Jackson's laboratory.

name: Pulsifer, Walter Trueman
description: "Red" came to college determined to win an "N.H." He has attended all sports including the cane rush. "Red" is a fluent talker on all subjects. In fact he is an authority on athletics, whist and law. In spite of his large vocabulary "Red" is one of the best hearted fellows you ever met. Understand him and he is a real friend and a hard worker. We are glad that "Red" has at last achieved his ambition and made a varsity letter in soccer. "Red" also holds the time honored title of chamberlain in Louis Bogon's palace.

name: Reed, Roger Allbee
motto: Fifteen two and a pair is four.
description: "Shorty's" twin comes from the north woods and isn't afraid to tell the world about it either. Just because he comes from the land of dog sleds is no sign that he is a "hick" and the kid is far from it. If you really think so let him show you the rudiments of anything from cribbage to basketball. Roger is also one of the charter members of the "Red-headed gallery" in Math. 1.5a.

name: Rhodes, Margaret Esther
description: "Mickey" is the girl to come to when you've cut your finger, for she just loves to bind up wounds for anyone. In fact her ambition was to be a nurse, but now she's more interested in learning to cook. Outside of writing to her MAN her favorite sports are swimming and skiing.

name: Rideout, Pearl
description: Pearl is one of those quiet and demure young ladies who are continually surprising their friends. Her favorite dissipation this year has been horseback riding and she has had some wild times with the animals. We heard her saying "Nice horsie, whoa ... stop, darn you," to a horse one afternoon just before she was rescued by the pride of the math department.

name: Roberts, Samuel Woodbury
description: "Sam," known only as Woodbury in his home town, came to college and made his abode at the Hotel Pettee. He was driven out by the Pettee Block fire and then went into seclusion at a private residence. Before entering New Hampshire, "Sam" took a twelve-year course in Agriculture at the Roberts Farm at Wakefield and he still studies there during the summers. "Sam's" worst habit is flunking exams - with 95 or more.

name: Robinson, Frederick Lebaron
clubs: Battalion Sergeant-Major R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Robbie" besides being an Editor-in-Chief, a brave soldier, and a fish peddler, is quite a musician. He played the "Victrola" his Freshman year and is now playing a stringed instrument. His favorite selection is "On To Manchester." We have heard "Robbie" enjoy his pipe and chewing gum. The number of his racer is related to 7-20-4. "Freddie" makes collections of junk, birds and animals. We once caught him with a "Hawk" on the proverbial string and later with a "Bull." We believe said "Bull" is still in his possession.

name: Rodden, Clement James
description: "Clem" is our best chemist, but nevertheless he still drips his courses. "Clem" came here to get married but he gives the co-eds no time. His week-ends were spent in journeying to Augusta until he obtained his second marriage license - for what nobody knows.

name: Rolfe, Mary Florence
motto: She may look sober; she may look shy, But she's full of it - 'twixt you and I.
description: A loud "Shhh!" is heard and a head is thrust through a crack in the door. "Honest, kids, you'll have to be more quiet or I'll give you a black mark!" - this, accompanied by a shade of a grin usually comes next. But then, quiet proctor isn't altogether the most enviable position on the campus; so we'll forgive our "Flap" for the black marks, and reputation of being a person who never gets angry at anything, anytime, anywhere. This even temperament has inspired great envy in her "hot-headed friends." Although "Pennycook" is but a small dot on the map, we'll have to admit that it must be a good place to have produced such a person as "Flap."

name: Rowe, Bernice Lydia
motto: I on the opposite side will be.
description: We always wondered why Bern never took up debating - she would have been an unqualified success. She has been described as "that quiet little girl whose wicked roommate taught her to swear." Bern is the "fixit" of her floor in Smith Hall. She remodeled the entire lighting system of one room with a fingernail file once - and it worked too.

name: Sanborn, Victor Paul
description: "Chuck" has the enviable record of only spending two week-ends in Durham his first year. He chartered trains for Boston, Portland, Auburn, Higgins Beach, Adams House and Warrenington Drug. But Chuck remarks: "Them days have gone forever." He has been subject to bad attacks of chronic puppy-love and we fear that now it has developed into a more severe case of its inevitable antecedent. We await the outcome. Scarcely seeing "Chuck" nowadays, we are unable to publish the latest developments of his last attack.

name: Schlenkir, Frank Scott
motto: All great men are eccentric.
description: "Torchy" was blessed with red hair, also a name, the pronunciation of which has defied the efforts of many learned men. This immediately exempted him from quizzing in class, with the resulting acquire-ment of a reputation for possessing marked talent and mental ability. In his social activities (most of them in certain nearby cities) he has exerted himself to live up to his physical qualifications of the original "Flaming Youth." Yet after overlooking his few eccentricities we discover a very earnest young "chemiker" who takes great pride in exhibiting the numerous acid holes in his trousers.

name: Scribner, Caryl Edith
description: There is only one junior that knows more about herself than anyone else does, and that's Caryl. She is so quiet and well behaved that we can't find a single bit of scandal to tell you about. She used to live in Boscawen, but that town disappeared from the map when she left for Durham, so now she is registered from Contoocook.

name: Sentner, Robert Verrill
description: "Lefty" travels to and from Dover every day. He started out to become a "chemiker" but after one year of this he decided he would train himself so that in the future he might revise the laws of economics. He is now an "eco" shark with this aim in view. His pastimes are spent by selling dry goods in a prominent Dover clothing store. Anyone who knows "Lefty" will admit that he has a great future and nothing but success can ever come to him.

name: Sharples, Robert Edmond
description: This winsome lad with wavy hair, has made many a drawing in Schramm's care. For "Bob" is an artist of no mean merit; proof of it is in room 355 DeMerritt. But drawing is not all he can do, for he hits his Math and M.E. too. However we've noticed his air now has altered, and we fear the poor boy has faltered. For the better it would seem though by the looks of his smile, and instead of his art he now raves about "her" all the while. For we think it's a "she" if a thought we may express, and her name might be Mary, if we can rightly guess. In the days of the future you'll no doubt hear of "Bob's" works of art for his work is sincere. Then, friends, let us rise and say right here: That you'll never succeed unless you're sincere.

name: Shepard, Rachel Elizabeth
description: "Ray" isn't quite as angelic a little girl as she looks. If you don't believe it look at her again. Someone once told her that the night air would give her curly hair, so ever since then she's diligently been trying to acquire it. Ray intends to be a French teacher sometime but her real ambition is to be a globe trotter. She is naturally bright so she never has to study.

name: Simmons, Emily Blanchard
motto: Does your Mother know you're out?
description: Demps once heard a policeman sing this song — 'member Demps? Demps had about five roommates her first year and was converted to the life of single cussedness which she has lived ever since. She keeps on the sweet side of the Y. W. — she sells the candy. Our Demps tried to ride a horse once but she prefers a Ford — it has brakes.

name: Simpson, Lloyd Atherton
motto: Simp, the student, breezed through his first year hitting his courses with a 90.
description: He lived in the famous Barracks B and did not give the outside world much of his time. During his second year bumming rides and co-eds made it rather hard for him to come through with the marks of the previous year. But now he gives all his time to the girl back in Concord. None like the home girl, he says.

name: Sleeper, Cleveland Jr.
motto: Sleeper, The Mystic Marvel, years from now the shingle on a great business establishment will announce to the world.
description: Those brown, interesting, hypnotic, dancing eyes and that soft, easy, persuasive voice assisted by a pompous dignity and gracious air of mysticism have made Sleepy the leading seer of his day. He gave promise of this great career in college days, by his remarkable seance at a football rally. Sleepy has had a great time in college. His keen mind and ready wit have helped him. His informal manner and impossible dress have made him a favorite with his fellow students. Laugh and the world laughs with you or at you — worry and you are crazy, is Sleepy's motto.

name: Smalley, Frederick Christopher
motto: Three years ago, Freddie stepped off the local from Dover, searching for a place to show his brains.
description: Morrill Hall being nearest the station, he went in there and Dean Taylor made him an Aggie. His aforementioned brains and ready smile soon made him friends with everyone. He is a good sport and a very fine fellow even though he does come from Dover. He spends quite a little of his time with the women and his special weakness is a blonde sister of a poultryman.

name: Smith, Charlotte Marie
description: Charlotte is a very capable young lady to say the least. We are willing to wager that she could take that Ford to pieces and remodel it if occasion should demand the performance. There is no doubt whatever that she can drive it very efficiently — and she plays hockey with the same degree of skill. Her biggest fault is that she never gives anything but her books any time!
