name: Smith, Claire Elizabeth
motto: A horse, a horse, my kingdom for a horse,
description: is Claire's favorite line of Shakespeare, and she knows how to ride when she finds one too. "Smitty" is studying for her "Batchelder's" degree and we feel sure she'll get it. She is majoring in German — police dogs. The "pride of Center Sandwich" thinks she gets picked on, but that's nothing. She does her own share of rumpus raising. No one would ever surmise that Claire had sung in the church choir, now would they?

name: Smith, Dorothy Tuck
motto: Dot, for pete's sake explain this darn zoo. to me
description: — and Dot explains — about six times a day on an average. Dot is our rock of refuge when we get in over our mental depth in a zoo course, because, although she doesn't look especially studious, she knows more zoo than the rest of us can ever realize exists. And her knowledge isn't confined exclusively to that subject, either.

name: Smith, Evelyn Hope
description: "Eve" doesn't make much noise in the dorm, but when she gets on a tennis court, oh my. Tennis and skating are her specialties. She has many friends and not one of them can find a word to say against her. Eve will make some man a wonderful wife.

name: Smith, Langdon Cornwell
description: Proctor Smith! "Parson" has with the help of "Freddy" Waite held the job of keeping the boys from raising the roof on the third floor of Fairchild for two years. "Parson" has proven his character and physique in several lines. He has held a place on the track squad, on the cross country team and in basketball. One of his principal courses is taught by certain co-eds. He sure is a heart-breaker. He holds a position on the social committee and the success of the Sophomore Hop is credited to "Parson." He is an all-round fellow and everyone admires him.

name: Smith, Maurice Basil
motto: Am I not right Mr. Smith?
description: The largest stockholder of the Busted & Maimed Railroad, rode into town from North Hampton on a pass, and has been "rode" free gratis ever since. He even ventured so far from home as to go to Michigan one summer. Ask "Smithy" if there are any good-looking women in the "far west." Basil has two hobbies, Military Science and M. E. Laboratory. He has not been able to decide which to major in but at present he seems to favor the latter.

name: Smith, Robert Elbridge
description: "Bob" is one of those quiet fellows who accomplishes lots of things but who is nevertheless little in the public eye. He is something of a card player but not so much of a singer as artist, although his actions indicate that he has some ambitions along all of these lines. His calm disposition is scarcely ruffled except when in some very deep argument with his roommates as to who should sweep the floor.

name: Southmayd, Clarendon Lester
motto: Yeth thur
description: C. L. comes from the one and only city of Franklin, N. H., and although he hates to admit it he occasionally does tell the boys all about the "big town." The song entitled, "Oh how I hate to get up in the morning," was made for "Southy," although one morning he was up at seven o'clock, but that was because he forgot to go to bed the night before. A detail of scientists have been searching for some time but to date "Southy" is the only one found who admitted that he enjoyed descriptive geometry.

name: Spillane, Charles Jeremiah
motto: Ask 'Buck' how many railroad ties there are between Durham and Newmarket. Or ask him, if it is not better to be late to an eight o'clock than not to go at all.
description: Commuting every day from that metropolis five miles south of College, he is a great help to the co-eds who commute. They say he is the sheik of Newmarket but we are not sure. "Buck" is a fine sort of a fellow and you never meet him when he has not that broad smile to greet you. He is a plugger both out and inside of the class-room, and we all look forward to "Buck's" success.

name: Stone, Fred Byron
motto: Early to bed and early to rise, for he has to catch the commuters' special every morning.
description: Fred has set out to be an engineer, and if he continues he is sure to succeed. If some of the radio enthusiasts in Dover would consult "Stonie" and another certain Dover commuter they might remedy some of the noises of the air. They have a private wave across the city and usually intercept those used by others. "Freddy" is a good-natured chap and liked by all.

Fred has set out to be an engineer, and if he continues he is sure to succeed. If some of the radio enthusiasts in Dover would consult "Stonie" and another certain Dover commuter they might remedy some of the noises of the air. They have a private wave across the city and usually intercept those used by others. "Freddy" is a good-natured chap and liked by all.

name: Tappan, Thomas Capron
motto: Bohunk
description: Such is the war cry from Derry, and as yet it is frequently heard in the halls of engineering, so beware! This loyal son of Pinkerton rolled into town in a Ford, model 1704, and lo and behold, two of the tires were blown up and only one cylinder was missing. No one who ever rode in "Rubbatub" would ever forget his ride. This would-be engineer was one of "Doc's" star pupils in 10a and his studies are the least of his troubles. His major course is the fair sex. Ask him about the women, not the woman.

name: Tatarcuk, Albert Joseph
motto: It was in the fall of '23 as the prophets used to say, that 'Al' decided to light the world - electrically and personally. Yea.
description: His success in the former is unquestioned, and as to the latter we refer you to the more deadly of the species in... Where? Oh, yes, in Nashua of course. But from the path of knowledge he is often apt to stray, for once I actually found him in Hampton far away. To those of us who know our "Al" he is a friend indeed, and we hope that his smile will always appear when we are in need of it.

name: Thomas, Janette
high school: Gardner High School, Durham, N. H.
motto: We often wish we were the post-master's niece as 'Janey' is, if that would insure us more mail. However, don't think for a minute that this is the only asset this miss from Templeton has. Anyone who knows her will tell you of her unfailing good-nature, her sudden flashes of dry humor, and above all, her friendliness.

name: Thompson, Wilbur Emmons
high school: Tilton Seminary, Wilmot Flat, N. H.
motto: She's pretty to walk with, And witty to talk with, And pleasant too, to think on.
description: "Tom" left Wilmot Flat to partake of the science of agriculture in the renowned halls of learning. He agrees that before he graduates he is going to be an expert in his line—picking buds on century plants. His conversation is limited. He says that talk is a waste of time unless it is to the fairer sex. He must have a reason, although we are unable to identify her. His laboratory is the college woods. He is a very conscientious student and we know some day he will grow forests wholesale.

name: Tibbetts, Elizabeth Fales
motto: She's pretty to walk with, And witty to talk with, And pleasant too, to think on.
description: Who can deny it? Lee has charmed us many times by her art, her graceful Pierrette dancing, the Charleston and clogging, and still she finds time for co-education. The fact that she is a member of Casque and Casket proves her ability in still another line. She has been known to remark, "One should not sacrifice good times for such trivial things as studying," and she is always ready for a good time.

name: Tobey, Louise
motto: We'd like to discover the secret of 'Tobey's' scholastic ability as she never seems to be bothered with studying. Like the elephant's child of which Kipling wrote, she has an insatiable curiosity. Another characteristic is her devotion (?) to her room-mate. The other evening we overheard this conversation. 'Will you go to the movies with Lu and me?' 'I can't, I'm going with Tobey.' 'Let's all go.' 'Oh no, Tobey and Lu are mad!'

name: Trombly, Napoleon Arthur
description: Picture if you can the great "Nap" seated in the gutter of a prominent Newmarket thoroughfare on a warm summer's day, trying to shoot horse-flies with a fourth of July blank cartridge revolver. It is true, ladies and gentlemen, every word of it. Hundreds of little children were gathered around to watch and people hung out of windows to gaze silently upon him. Suddenly the air was rent with a mighty shout, "Hey, Godbeer, I killed another one." And the populace burst into wild joy, crying, "Ah, Le General: Napoleon Trombly."

name: Van Allen, Albert David
clubs: Alpha Sigma Phi, University of Penn. '26
Varsity Track at University of Penn.
description: His Long Island brogue, his infectious smile, his ability on a cinder track, his personality which sticks out all over him, made "Van" popular with all the boys and girls until he met Marion. He is still popular with all the boys and would be with the girls, — if he could see any of them.

name: Varney, Gilbert Leslie
description: "Barney" hailed from the tall timbers of Plymouth, three years ago, determined to become an engineer; but soon decided that he couldn't give Doc. Slobin's courses any time and changed to Agricultural lines. He is now applying his engineering ability to figuring the exact number of tooth-picks to be derived from a six-foot log, where x equals the length. His ambition is to exterminate the green junipers in the South Sea Islands so that the natives can grow more pineapples. He slings hash at Grant's for a pastime and watches the mail for those large, daily epigrams. Cheer up, "Barney," that girl from Concord still loves you.

name: Waite, Frederick
description: "Freddie" is a chemiker and is rapidly absorbing knowledge under the direction of "King" James and "Hemie" Fogg. Besides that he holds the position of assistant proctor on the third deck of Fairchild and sees to it that law and order are maintained in that section of the campus. If "Freddie's" application is any measure of his achievement, he need have no fear of his success.

name: Gardner, Howard Wales
description: The "Mayflower" was almost the ruination of "Pash." He and "Kim," the drug store athlete, would start for Portland, Enfield, or Burlington or even Raymond at noon and expect to be back in time for classes at eight o'clock the next morning. It was only a hope, not an expectation. And the funny part of it is they would have thirty cents between them, four gallons of gas, and a good line of "Bla." Nevertheless "Pash" usually pulls down an average of between seventy-five and eighty-five so we can't kick.

name: Todd, Bryce Wallace
description: He says it's "Wally" but the boys call him "Fatty." His hobbies include playing the traps, the women and the races. Green Acres, Exeter, and an old fashioned song called "Sweet Rosie O'Grady" have ruined his faith in mankind. If you see him tearing around in a Hudson sedan, he's just trying to regain it; - his faith.

name: Ruth, Webber
description: "Fritz" comes from the wilds of Maine but you wouldn't guess it. She doesn't care for hockey or basketball; hiking and snowshoeing are her specialties. "Fritz" will ride in anything from a Ford truck to a "coupe" and never object. She has a wonderful drag in the sociology department but is now thinking of majoring in forestry.

name: Irene, Martin Wentworth
description: Did you ever ask Irene to read your handwriting? Don't do it because all your little hidden faults will be brought to light, and she will know all about you. You might ask her about her social work in Dover, though. Irene reads everything, even Jurgen, and her great ambition is to be a newspaper reporter. There are rumours, however, that she would make a much better free verse writer.

name: White, Elizabeth Alice
motto: The music in my heart I bore, Long after it was heard no more.
description: Betty's spirit is enthusiastic, and even if the tune is a few notes off — what's the difference? There's some advantage in being able to sing all the songs you know to one tune. Betty enjoys the Library — she says you can find so many interesting things there. We've noticed she brings some of them back to Smith Hall and "parks" them on the front steps. She does study, though we often wonder when she finds the time. Betty proved to be one of those gems whose quiet lustre needs to be sought out, because it is too modest to show its real radiance. When THE GRANITE hit a "snag" Betty immediately proffered her services and did her work exceedingly well.

name: White, William Prescott
description: "Willie" hails from the big town of Rye, "down by the sea." He is known down there as a mighty hunter and fisherman. He once had an ambition to become an athlete, but finally decided to give it up and become a soccer player instead. At school he spends most of his time at Conant Hall. However, with all his little faults, he's a fine fellow and once you know him you will ask for no better fellow.

name: Whyte, Russell Paul
description: "Russ" sure is a knockout. If you cannot take our word for it ask anyone who has met him in the varsity "squared ring." He does other things just the same way that he hits: — straight from the shoulder. Although we have never seen "Russ" play baseball we feel sure that he is working hard at college preparing himself to make a "home run" after he gets the foolscap. Congratulations, "Russ," only please leave that uppercut in the equipment room with the gloves.

name: Wiggin, Stanley Lyman
description: "Stan" came down from the great city of Gonic with a "rep" as a basketball player. He made his numerals pronto, but his second and third years he figured he could make more money playing "pro," so now he may be Mister Sullivan of South Berwick, or "Flash" Smith from Portland. But anyway, whoever he may be, he still is the same old "Wig:" the boy from home, who can ring 'em from any angle of the floor.

name: Wilcox, Clifton
motto: "Clif" blew down from Concord one September day back in 1922, after having told the Governor how to run things at the Capitol. Apparently "things" did not run according to directions or else too many week-end trips to Boston had to be explained for "Clif" decided to stay at home his Junior year in order to teach and for other reasons not mentioned. Now he can say with Kipling, "And I learned about women from her."

name: Willard, Herbert Andrew
motto: Would you think that this quiet, soft-spoken lad was addicted to tooting his own horn? (Don't tell anyone, he plays in the band.) Although extremely shy, he has had several "dates" lately. It is said that the first was arranged by one of the fair sex, being attracted, probably, by his blond hair, blue eyes and "big car." "Herb" is a fine fellow and we all wish him the best of luck.

name: Willard, Mervin Edwin
motto: "Jess" while only one-sixteenth the size of his namesake could give the big boy a lesson in gameness and fighting spirit. Although discouraged by the coach because of his slight physique, "Jess" stuck and has been a great help to the Cross Country Team. While in Durham he has hid the light of his social life under a basket, but we are told he is quite popular with the ladies in Temple.

name: Willgeroth, George Edward
motto: Outgrowing the home nest, "Eddie" left his burrow in the hills and set out for Durham to get an "Education." Being near Henniker he decided to take poultry as his major. But he is still so true to that girl he left behind that if he even looks at a co-ed, his thoughts start wandering back to the hills of old New Hampshire.

name: Williams, Chester Elmer
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: This handsome lad with azure blue eyes, is "Chet" and none other and from Portsmouth too. When first he landed upon our campus he chose E.E. as a course, while the co-eds said "vamp us." But of the fair co-eds this lad would have none, why he e'en goes over the hill to his classes on the run. In his first year with us, from the Pettee Block fire he emerged with pajamas as his sole attire. But not to be daunted by this slap in the face, he stuck to E.E. and now is setting a dazzling pace. And when "Chet" comes around he's cheerful and gay; let's hope he'll soon have an income tax to pay.

name: Williamson, Clayton Marnoch
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: "Willie" started out to be a '26 man but since then his mind has been changed and now we claim him as one of us. He comes from that nearby city of Dover, but we won't hold that against him. He has shown himself worthy on the track squad and on the soccer team. "Willie" is a good all-around man and we welcome him to our ranks. When he leaves as an engineer he intends to build a subway between here and Dover for the benefit of all commuters. We wish you the best of luck, "Willie."

name: Wilmot, Manly
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: There is a saying that all fat men are good-natured. Surely "Bill" is no exception. He always sees the funny side of everything even when the joke's on his room-mates. With "Bill's" great musical talent and his wonderful personality, we expect that he'll make a hit and success in the teaching profession after his graduation. Here's hoping. Good luck, "Bill."

name: Wood, Katherine Alma
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Katherine, a seemingly quiet young lady, joined us last year, straight from New London, Conn., where she had been strenuously pursuing the academics at Conn. College. Ask her if it is co-ed, and see what happens. She plans to teach until 1928 - after that she won't say. Perhaps that Dartmouth banner that occupies such a prominent place in her room could disclose the secret.

name: Woodman, Margaret Cushman
motto: Here's to the fair maiden with the dusky hair and eyes.
description: To know Midge - that means a lot. After two years at Congreve Midge decided she would like a change so she moved to the palatial Crescent Apartments. There she "Lords" it over the freshies - as President of course. Midge is fond of reading. In fact she can quote anything from Tolstoy to Fannie Hurst. Her chuckle may be identified anywhere on the campus - so watch out, Midge.

name: Wright, Linwood Arlon
description: Enter "Wild Linny, the Sanford Tiger." Lin isn't really as rough as he looks, and he assures us that all will go well if we don't kid him about the home town. His one great failing - next to his books - is his liking for cards, and he is often found teaching Langdell the fundamentals of bridge. It is rumored he has a way with the women and when some sweet young thing gazes wistfully into his eyes of blue - ??? The story must go no farther. So that's that - gentlemen - that's that.

name: Bruce, Edgar Brown
description: "Ed" has commuted thus far but that can hardly be held against him. He has won the ski cross country cup the one time he went after it and is a consistent pitcher for the baseball team. Although he is independent he is a hard worker and a corking good friend. He has been beaten around Milton for several years now and maybe that is the reason he commutes. The class of '27 knows that "Ed" will be of as much service to social science as he has been to his Alma Mater.
